metadata.title:
BROAD WILL
E. HAV[ A T[5T

Contractor Arrested for
Violation of 8.HOur


Ordinance.

pages.raw_text:
The bitter dispute between Con-
tractor James c. Broad and the Fed
eral Labor union. backed by Mayor
Boyd. over the contractors refusal
to pay his men, working on municipal
contracts, 32.25 for an eight-hour day
eliminated in the arrest Of Broad
Immediately previous to the noon
hour. ,


The warrant for his arrest was
sworn TO- by. c. w. Dickinson, charg-
ing Broad with unlawfully violating
Ordinance No. A Ill, and the amend
ments thereto, entitled. IAN ordinance
to establish the hours to constitute
days work on all municipal con-
struction or such work done by con-
tract or subcontract and providing
for the wages to be paid laborers en-
ployed in doing the same. and pro-
viding penalties for its violation"


Immediately after his arrest At
torneys Cullen and Dudley applied for
writ Of habeas corpus. which was
granted by Judge Richardson, return
able at 1 o'clock. this afternoon, and
Broad has now Joined issue to deter
mine the legality of the city ,ordi-
nance providing for union rate Of
pay on city work.


1t will be remembered that Mayor
Boyd refused to sign an important
contract let to Broad on the ground
that the clause providing for the
payment of 32.25 for eight hours
work had not been inserted by the
board of public works and sent the
same back to be rectified. The con-
tractor on his part refused to accept
the contract with the clause inserted,
stating that he would pay $2.25 for
nine hours work only. Meanwhile
be has lost no time since he was
arrested to have the legal aspect of
the case come up for a hearing and
a final decision to be rendered as to
his liability under the ordinance by
tho courts.
